semanticCore: Weakness, fragility, fading, feebleness, or bone decay
quranicRelevance: Physical weakness of aging bones (Wahana - 'wahana al-'athmu minnee'), weakness of spider houses ('awhana al-buyooti labaytu al-'ankaboot'), and spiritual feebleness/discouragement ('wa la tahinoo wa la tahzanoo').

keyDerivations.meaning: To grow weak, feeble, lose strength, or decay

figurativeOrRareUsages: لَبَيْتُ الْعَنْكَبُوتِ لَوْ كَانُوا يَعْلَمُونَ (29:41): The house of the spider (the flimsiest/weakest house, representing the fragility of seeking security in false gods).
discardedNoise: The physical speed of spider silk weaving.
summaryEn: The root denotes weakness and fragility, framing the natural decline of old age (Wahana), warning against spiritual defeatism, and utilizing the spider's web metaphor.
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على الوهن والوهاد البنيوي، واصفاً وهن عظام الهرم (وهن العظم)، ووهن شباك العنكبوت المجازي للشرك، وناهياً عن الضعف الإيماني.
rawText: أَوْهَنَهُ 4 He, or it, weakened him: [rendered him languid, languid and faint, or lax in the joints; enervated him, unnerved him, or broke his energy; rendered him cowardly: (see وَهَنَ:)] (S, Msb, K:) and 🡻وَهَنَهُ, (S, Mgh, Msb, K,) but the former is the better, (Msb,) and 🡻وَهَّنَهُ. (S, K.) تَوَهُّنٌ Weak, languid, unable to rise: see عُدَوَآءُ. عِرْقُ الوَاهِنَةِ The cephalic vein: see فَلِيقٌ. وَهَنَ 1 He was, or became, weak, or infirm, in an affair, and in operation, and in body; (Msb:) and so said of a bone: (Bd, and Jel in xix. 3:) and he was, or became, languid, languid and faint, or lax in the joints; (TA, Bd in iii. 140;) enervated, unnerved, or broken in energy; (Bd, ubi suprà;) cowardly. (TA, Jel in iii. 140.) === See also 4. وَهَّنَ 2 see 4. وَهْنٌ The period about midnight; (S, K;) or the time after an hour, or a short period, (سَاعَة,) of the night: (JK, K, TA:) or when the night is departing. (S.) See إِنْىٌ. وَهْنَانَةٌ I. q. وَنَاةٌ, q. v.; and see أَنَاةٌ.
